display_name: Fabric-wide VXLAN loss often traces to a single flapping NVE source loopback, not many VNI problems
context: Multiple VNIs or many hosts behind a leaf lose connectivity at the same time in a VXLAN/EVPN fabric
observation: Simultaneous reachability loss across many VNIs and tenants on one leaf, often correlated with brief recoveries
naive_interpretation: Many separate VNI/tenant problems, or a control-plane (EVPN) outage affecting numerous routes at once
correct_interpretation: Every VNI on a leaf shares one VXLAN tunnel endpoint, sourced from the NVE source-interface (a loopback). If that loopback — or the IGP route to it, or the underlay path — flaps, the VTEP goes down and all overlay traffic for that leaf drops together. The blast radius looks like dozens of problems but the root cause is a single shared dependency. Check NVE/VTEP state and the source loopback reachability before chasing individual VNIs.
why: RFC 7348 defines the VTEP as the encap/decap point; all VNIs on a device share the same source VTEP IP
RFC 8365 uses EVPN as the control plane but forwarding still depends on the underlay route to the remote VTEP being present
A flapping loopback or its IGP advertisement withdraws the VTEP reachability for every overlay simultaneously, producing correlated multi-VNI loss
The correlation in time across unrelated tenants is the tell that the failure is a shared underlay/VTEP dependency, not per-VNI

confirmation_commands.purpose: Check whether VTEP peers are flapping or down, which points to a shared source/underlay issue
Confirm the NVE source-interface (loopback) and its operational state
Verify the underlay/IGP route to the source loopback is stable rather than flapping

traps: Opening separate investigations per VNI when all of them share one VTEP source and went down together
Blaming the EVPN control plane when the underlay route to the VTEP is what actually withdrew
Not checking the source loopback and its IGP advertisement, the single point all overlays depend on
Missing the time correlation across tenants that distinguishes a shared-dependency failure from many independent ones

sources.title: RFC 7348 — Virtual eXtensible Local Area Network (VXLAN)
RFC 8365 — A Network Virtualization Overlay Solution Using EVPN
